Name: AggieSat Laboratory
Relevance: Student-led program designing and building CubeSats with multiple successful orbital missions.

Name: Sounding Rocketry Team (SRT)
Relevance: Competes in high-powered rocketry competitions like IREC and Spaceport America Cup.

Name: TAMU Rocket Engine Design (RED)
Relevance: Focuses on the development and testing of liquid-propellant rocket engines.

Name: SEDS - Texas A\&M
Relevance: Local chapter of Students for the Exploration and Development of Space focusing on industry networking and projects.

Name: Land Air and Space Robotics (LASR) Lab
Relevance: Researches robotic sensing and proximity operations for space and planetary applications.

Name: Aerospace Human Systems Lab (AHSL)
Relevance: Specializes in spacesuit design and human-system integration for microgravity and planetary surfaces.

Name: ASTRO Center
Relevance: Technology development center for space sensors thermal systems and power.

Name: Munnerlyn Astronomical Instrumentation Lab
Relevance: Builds advanced instrumentation for major telescopes such as the Giant Magellan Telescope.

Name: Mitchell Institute for Fundamental Physics and Astronomy
Relevance: Conducts research on dark matter cosmology and the early universe.

Name: National Aerothermochemistry and Hypersonics Laboratory
Relevance: Leading research center for high-speed flight and hypersonic propulsion.

Name: Aggie Rover
Relevance: Student team designing planetary rovers for NASA competitions.

Name: Space Food Research Facility
Relevance: Processes and tests shelf-stable foods for NASA missions and the ISS.

Name: Cyclotron Institute
Relevance: Provides heavy-ion beams for testing radiation effects on space electronics.

Name: High Altitude Balloon Club (HABC)
Relevance: Launches and recovers high-altitude balloon payloads for near-space research.

Name: Advanced Vertical Flight Laboratory (AVFL)
Relevance: Researches VTOL flight for applications including Mars exploration.

Name: AIAA TAMU Chapter
Relevance: Professional organization hosting aerospace technical projects and guest speakers.

Name: Space Engineering Research Center (SERC)
Relevance: Interdisciplinary center facilitating space-related technology development across TAMU.

Name: SAE Aero Design
Relevance: Designs and builds heavy-lift UAVs applicable to aerospace engineering fundamentals.

Name: Laboratory of Space Systems and Optomechanics (LASSO)
Relevance: Develops precision sensors for mass change and geodesy on satellite platforms.

Name: Plasma Engineering and Diagnostics Lab
Relevance: Conducts research on plasma flows and diagnostics for aerospace and energy.

Name: Aerospace Vehicle Systems Institute (AVSI)
Relevance: Cooperative research center involving industry and government on aerospace vehicle systems.

Name: Remote Sensing and GIS Lab
Relevance: Uses satellite-derived data for Earth informatics and environmental monitoring.

Name: Texas A\&M Spaceflight Operations
Relevance: Provides training and support for mission operations and human spaceflight activities.

Name: Aggie Aerospace Women in Engineering (AAWE)
Relevance: Professional support group for students in space-adjacent engineering disciplines.

Name: TAMU Microgravity Team
Relevance: Participated in NASA's Reduced Gravity Education Flight Program conducting zero-G experiments.
